body:
Another log: https://github.com/coder/coder/actions/runs/2314435722/attempts/1

Looks like it's hanging and running into the 3 minute timeout.

The risk here is that there may be an underlying issue that's causing this that could bite us later if we don't fix? But we really don't know how catastrophic the underlying cause actually is without investigation? @spikecurtis

flakes are bad 2 two reasons:

1. maybe there is a bug in our code that is the underlying cause of the flake
2. developers rerun the tests and get used to ignoring test failures

If we can't prioritize immediately investigating and fixing the issue (i.e. point 1 above doesn't worry us very much), we should disable the test to avoid point 2.

> flakes are bad 2 two reasons:
> 
> 1. maybe there is a bug in our code that is the underlying cause of the flake
> 2. developers rerun the tests and get used to ignoring test failures
> 
> If we can't prioritize immediately investigating and fixing the issue (i.e. point 1 above doesn't worry us very much), we should disable the test to avoid point 2.

seems like its probably at least worth an initial investigation to pinpoint a cause before we decide if it's okay to disable a test and/or not fix immediately.

Hey team! Please [add your planning poker estimate](https://app.zenhub.com/workspaces/Workspaces-V2-Hub-62019e46b7e29b0014a7f850/issues/coder/coder/1368?planning-poker) with ZenHub @dwahler @spikecurtis
@dwahler see if you can sort out the need for a fix on this one. We can evaluate after to see what prio this should be.
This looks like the same issue as #1348, just in a different test case.

issue.body:
TestAgent/ReconnectingPTY was making some fragile assumptions about the data returned from a `net.Conn`. This PR makes it a bit more robust.

The previous version was sending `echo test` via a PTY, and then expecting to see the string `test` in the output twice: once from the echoed command line, and once from the command output itself. But it was expecting them to be returned by two different calls to `Read()`, and there's no guarantee of that. 

The new version uses buffered I/O to read one line at a time, and expects to see at least one line that contains `echo test`, followed by at least one line that contains `test` but not `echo`. It also sleeps for 100ms to reduce the likelihood that the shell prompt test will be interleaved with the local echo of the command.

There's still some room for improvement here, because ideally the test would be controlling both ends of the PTY, instead of just assuming the user's shell behaves predictably.

Fixes #1348, fixes #1368
